segments.text: The burden of the word of the Lord to Israel by Malachi.
I have loved you.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Yet you say,
In what way have You loved us?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Was not Esau Jacob's brother?
Yet Jacob I have loved;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Esau I have hated, And laid waste his mountains and his heritage For the jackals of the wilderness.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: We have been impoverished, But we will return and build the desolate places
segments.speaker: Edom

segments.text: They may build, but I will throw down; They shall be called the Territory of Wickedness, And the people against whom the Lord will have indignation forever.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: Your eyes shall see,
The Lord is magnified beyond the border of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unspecified

segments.text: A son honors his father, And a servant his master. If then I am the Father, Where is My honor? And if I am a Master, Where is My reverence?
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: To you priests who despise My name. Yet you say,
In what way have we despised Your name?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: You offer defiled food on My altar,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But say,
In what way have we defiled You?
segments.speaker: Priests

segments.text: By saying, 'The table of the Lord is contemptible.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And when you offer the blind as a sacrifice, Is it not evil? And when you offer the lame and sick, Is it not evil? Offer it then to your governor! Would he be pleased with you? Would he accept you favorably?
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: But now entreat God's favor, That He may be gracious to us. While this is being done by your hands, Will He accept you favorably?
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: Who is there even among you who would shut the doors, So that you would not kindle fire on My altar in vain? I have no pleasure in you, Nor will I accept an offering from your hands.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: From the rising of the sun, even to its going down, My name shall be great among the Gentiles; In every place incense shall be offered to My name, And a pure offering; For My name shall be great among the nations.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: But you profane it, In that you say, 'The table of the Lord is defiled; And its fruit, its food, is contemptible.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: You also say, 'Oh, what a weariness!' And you sneer at it. And you bring the stolen, the lame, and the sick; Thus you bring an offering! Should I accept this from your hand?
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts

segments.text: But cursed be the deceiver Who has in his flock a male, And takes a vow, But sacrifices to the Lord what is blemished— For I am a great King, And My name is to be feared among the nations.
segments.speaker: The Lord of hosts
